item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will attempt what I can, I've got photoshop, that will probably do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even the most basic image editor — like MS Paint — has an option to resize images. Open your images in that and adjust them to be smaller, then save.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m not quite sure how to compress them though. If you know how you’re welcome to do so. Though, where could I learn to do that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoyed this a lot! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your images are all nearly 2 MB or so. This causes slow loading for people with slow connections and eats up unnecessary bandwidth for people with metered connections. Given the size of images in articles this resolution is more than the human eye can see anyway, compressing them considerably won't effect their appearance in your article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do you mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I’m glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the images on this could be downscaled at least a tad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good, I always love to see humanoid skips that fit into realer archetypes of people, and an influencer skip is a lovely idea!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the idea that she also "influences" the perception of reality of her followers, hence the bots?

Kind of. Basically everyone will think what she sees causes she posts it and it looks real. If they were to see what it really looked like, it would have its true appearance. But they took away her account which meant she started influencing personnel around her. They gave her the account back with bots so she thought she was influencing them so she’d stop influencing foundation personnel.

I’ll correct those later, thank you!

I’m glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this a great deal. I especially enjoyed the way the SCP engages with reality; it reminds me a great deal of early 2000s emo/the main character of My Immortal, but if she had an instagram. Is the idea that she also "influences" the perception of reality of her followers, hence the bots?

spelling mistake
spelling mistake

I did notice two typos: you wrote "plus and your damn pink jacket" which seems… odd, and "anitmemitic."

Dr. Chameleon being taken off the case seemed a bit odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see this published!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's an interesting concept but a few things don't work for me. All of the below is not meant to be harsh or dismissive, I really think you have potential here and I adore Just Girly Things. This one just didn't work for me as well.

Stuff
Close

I'm not sure I understand what's going on with Dr. Chameleon; the 'color changing skin' comment makes me believe there's some backstory here, but I haven't seen it. Either way, it distracted me and muddies the waters around whatever you're trying to do with the doctor's removal from the project.

There's too little exploration given to how this anomaly began. They have access to -1's former home, so presumably they can piece together [i]some[/i] kind of timeline.

The dialogue itself could be cleaned up as well. Here are some examples:

Dr. Chameleon

Why would they be referred to in official documentation by a nickname?

SCP-5098-1, allow me to clear this up for you. You are here to be contained due to anomalous properties, and not to have your account sponsored. You will only gain access to your account as-needed for testing purposes.

This is a bit torqued and difficult to follow without a close reading. It doesn't feel like natural speech. Something like "SCP-5098-1, allow me to clear this up: you are in secure containment because of your anomalous nature. You're not here to have your account sponsored, and you'll only receive access to it for testing purposes."

SCP-5098-1: An entity? What, like a ghost or something?
Dr. Chameleon: That is unimportant.

It seems kind of enormously important; the nature of the entity could inform containment and analysis. It also seems a bit odd that she jumps to 'ghost or something' from 'entity'. Entity doesn't typically have supernatural connotations in every day speech. I might try something like:

SCP-5098-1: An entity? Uh, what do you mean
Dr. Chameleon: An anomalous being, analogically similar to a ghost.
SCP-5098-1: Uh, what?

and et cetera going into the tirade about her photographs being normal, on the back of asserting person/standard-humanhood.

Dr. Chameleon: I'd disagree. Everyone is seeing things…different. This room doesn't have pink walls, they're solid metal! It's a small room with basic furnishings. You don't have a couch, or a TV. You have long, brown hair, and you are wearing an orange Foundation sweatshirt with grey sweat pants, plus and your damn pink jacket. You don't have short pink hair. You're not wearing fishnet leggings with boots and a skirt, or whatever. I can see that! Why the fuck can't anyone else anymore?

This is waaaay too much exposition from the doc, plus she's not wrong, what rule is she breaking? If she is affecting staff, that's a containment breach and is on the Foundation. Chameleon should probably be concerned about the fallout from that, I would think.

Dr. Chameleon: Really SCP-5098-1? Really? You don't have the right to be questioning me here.

A bit awkwardly phrased. "I'm asking the questions here" is cliche'd but probably better. A simple "Answer the question!" would be even better.

Dr. Chameleon: (Sighs) Why am I getting so worked up about this? You're the one that didn't want to believe all this! Why am I even talking to you about this? Now I'm even…acting…like…you.

Yea… why are they getting so worked up? That could probably be explored, if they're gonna get worked up and call it out.

Final thing:

SCP-5098-1 was provided with a standard Foundation-issue smartphone with access to its Instagram account once all its followers were tracked down, amnestied, and replaced with shell accounts operated by Foundation web bots. Containment procedures have been updated to reflect this change.

y tho?

Again, I really don't mean to trash your article and I hope you don't feel like I just vomited up a bunch of criticism. I really do think it could work with some polish. I'm no-voting for now, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s actually in no way connected to JGT in any form. But you’re not the first to make that mistake! It’s all good!

Those images are in the Creative Commons under the correct licenses meaning I can use them.

Yeah I know it looses steam but I couldn’t think of a better ending.

I’m glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And another addition to the Girly Chronicle! (is this in any way connected to Just Girly Things?)

What we have seen here is a portrait of annoyingness and frustration.

Though I admit, it loses steam once it gets to the description of what happens when you do not have the other half of the SCP wear the jacket. But that can be easily fixed.

In other words…
+1

Expect a drawing (once I figure out how it should look like)

(P.S. : Wonder who are the people in the photo?
Just assurance questions.
People can sue for the unasked for use of their face)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I can do that! Thank you for alerting me of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure I get this but it seems pretty cool!

Would you consider increasing the bottom margin of the second video log? The log itself and the disciplinary notice from Lunar are right up against each other and it looks a tad strange.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much for letting me know.

Also I’m glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Odd article. Enjoyed it enough for a +1.

Minor spelling thing
– hide block

"Affects" in third-to-last paragraph should be "effects"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you don’t mind, could you explain what you mean by contrived?

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: The drop the Dr thing was kinda to get 5098 to comply more easily. I’ll add more to this comment later, I have a huge headache rip

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interviews don't work at all for me, and while the conceit is original it feels a little contrived in how the effect plays out.

item.title: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded:
This is a novel an interesting concept which is an often rare and precious thing. I think a lot of people are afraid to tackle social media / more modern societal concepts in their SCP's because of potential audience and tone mis-matching but this piece does a fairly good job of pursuing that.

I will say that your lampshading of the SCP's conduct is necessary and used fairly effectively, but there are stretches of the dialogue that are stilted and difficult to love.

As a personal preference, I don't think that the Foundation would dehumanize an anomaly by referring to it only as an SCP. Yes, I get the reason and historicity as to why, but it doesn't strike me as practical and personal especially when the interviewer is getting comfortable and saying "Drop the Dr."

Having the interviewer be named Chameleon is striking me as odd but I am being told "that's definitely a name people have". I'm not going to disagree with that assertion and I don't hold it against this piece.

Overall, this is novel and makes for a memorable character in spite of my hangups about the dialogue. I can comfortably +1 this.

item.title: author post
item.content:encoded:
Reviewers/beta readers/formatters:
Greyve
Mew-ltiverse
XilasCrowe
HammerMaiden
PyschLantern
TrustyOlValet2

I probably forgot some


Hi. I had this idea so I went with it. I hope it's ready, it seemed ready. Greyve and to an extent Mew really helped with story direction.

Picture credit:
By Paweł Suwarski, Poland - Photo collection of Paweł Suwarski, Public Domain, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=2052098

https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Female_artificial_red_hair&filefrom=Happy+Hat+for+St.+Pat.jpg#/media/File:Makeup_.jpg

By Vanessatevesti - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=28817055

I have edited this image by changing the hair and adding a jacket. Only the edits belong to me. The original belongs entirely to the person credited above.

The rest of the photos are mine. The jacket is my jacket and the other pictures are all ones i took at places. It's my hand holding that Disney milkshake (which tasted terrible btw) woooooo

Edit: yes I know the pictures are formatted horrifically on mobile I’m trying to fix that please give me time
